findings: Distinctive editorial art direction: oversized serif typography, deep green, citrus accents, fine-line motifs, and confident asymmetry.
Strong investment through the full page: product walkthrough, nutrition dashboard, testimonial metrics, conversion panel, and complete footer all receive bespoke treatment.
Three food/lifestyle photographs function as product evidence through overlays, scores, timing, and planning context—not decoration alone.
Desktop composition shifts intelligently between split hero, card triptych, app-like planner, dark editorial feature, testimonial, and CTA.
Mobile is substantially recomposed: navigation collapses, planner becomes horizontally structured, imagery and data remain legible, and sections preserve their identity.

Minor deductions: several sections are vertically generous; repeated oversized serif headlines become predictable; the mobile 02–01–03 card order feels slightly arbitrary; some pale secondary text approaches low contrast.
Highly polished and exceptionally consistent, with disciplined typography, restrained greens, lime accents, rounded containers, and repeatable UI language.
Clear hierarchy and strong scanning throughout, though the centered hero and repeated three-column/card structures feel closer to a premium template than a singular editorial concept.
Lower-page execution remains complete and considered: process, nutrition guidance, weekly planner, proof, CTA, and footer are all fully designed.
Real food imagery is integrated into product demonstrations and metric overlays, but repeated use of the same bowl imagery reduces narrative breadth.
Mobile adaptation is clear and competent, with good stacking, readable content, and preserved interactive-looking modules.

Minor deductions: long stretches rely on similar cream backgrounds and centered containers; section rhythm is more uniform than surprising; the mobile sample-week module has visibly cramped meal-label alignment and clipped horizontal navigation.

confidence: High. All four full-page screenshots were inspected; Version A wins through stronger art direction, broader layout vocabulary, and more bespoke lower-page storytelling. Version B is more systemically consistent but less distinctive.
limitations: Still images cannot prove hover, animation, tab behavior, menu operation, focus states, or other interaction quality; no points were awarded for unverified behavior.
